analysis_scope: Identify non-memory-corruption CVEs from the collected bzip2/libbz2 dataset that still matter when porting the libbz2 library itself to Rust.
selection_rules: Keep CVEs whose root cause is not primarily memory corruption.
Keep CVEs that affect the libbz2 library engine or API-visible decompression behavior.
Exclude CVEs limited to bundled utilities or helper scripts because the current port target is the library, not the bzip2, bzip2recover, or bzexe executables.

relevant_cves.issue_class: algorithmic denial of service / infinite-loop handling
relevant_cves.summary: bzip2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (hard drive consumption) via a crafted bzip2 file that causes an infinite loop (a.k.a "decompression bomb").
relevant_cves.why_selected: This is a library-level decompression logic flaw, not a C-specific memory bug. A Rust reimplementation still needs explicit progress guarantees, loop termination conditions, and output-growth limits when decoding malformed streams.
rust_port_considerations: Reject malformed inputs that can cause non-terminating decompression.
Track decoder progress so each iteration either consumes input, produces bounded output, or errors out.
Consider configurable output or work limits for hostile inputs.

relevant_cves.summary: Integer overflow in the BZ2_decompress function in decompress.c in bzip2 and libbzip2 before 1.0.6 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted compressed file.
relevant_cves.why_selected: The original exploitability in C involved unsafe arithmetic inside the library decompressor, but the underlying bug class is arithmetic validation, not just raw memory corruption. In Rust, equivalent unchecked size math could still become a panic, mis-sized allocation, or incorrect bounds decision.
rust_port_considerations: Use checked arithmetic for all decoded lengths, selector counts, and buffer-size derivations.
Validate all format-derived counts before allocation or indexing.
Convert arithmetic overflow and impossible state transitions into deterministic decode errors.

excluded_non_memory_cves.reason: Command-line file-creation overwrite issue; not part of the libbz2 stream API.
Command-line decompression permission race; only relevant if the bzip2 utility is also reimplemented.
Archive utility permission/symlink handling issue; outside the in-memory library surface.
Command-line hard-link race during output permission changes; not a libbz2 library concern.
Affects the bzexe wrapper script rather than the library compressor/decompressor.

excluded_memory_corruption_cves.reason: Buffer over-read in library parsing logic. Important historical context, but excluded here because this file only tracks non-memory-corruption CVEs.
Use-after-free in bzip2recover. Also limited to a bundled utility rather than libbz2 itself.
Out-of-bounds write in the library decompressor. Excluded here because the issue class is memory corruption.
